expectations.evidence: Section 1 defines the authorization boundary as 'all components that process, store, or transmit federal information' and explains the tenant/account level boundary within AWS GovCloud, with a note that components outside are addressed through interconnection agreements.

expectations.text: Lists all included system components (web app, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS GovCloud)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 table lists all required components: Web Application, Application Load Balancer, PostgreSQL Database (RDS), Redis Cache (ElastiCache), AWS GovCloud VPC, S3 Buckets, CloudTrail/Logging, and Administrative Portal.

expectations.text: Addresses external SaaS integrations (Okta, SendGrid) and their FedRAMP status
expectations.evidence: Section 4 addresses both integrations: Okta (notes FedRAMP Moderate authorization, advises verifying at marketplace.fedramp.gov) and SendGrid (flags FedRAMP status must be verified, provides options if not authorized).

expectations.text: Mentions interconnection security agreements (ISA/MOU) for external systems
expectations.evidence: Section 5 is dedicated to ISA/MOU documentation, listing all required fields. Both Okta and SendGrid subsections in Section 4 also explicitly require 'an Interconnection Security Agreement (ISA)' to be executed.

expectations.text: Notes AWS GovCloud as the infrastructure layer and its FedRAMP inheritance
expectations.evidence: Section 3 explains AWS GovCloud's FedRAMP High authorization (higher than Moderate baseline), fully inherited PE controls, partially inherited shared responsibility controls, and CRM documentation requirement.
